articles.title: Energy stock Fujiyama Power Systems hits upper circuit following stock market rebound
articles.summary: Fujiyama Power Systems stock opened at ₹302 apiece today, lower than its previous closing price of ₹304.60 on Monday. Here's company's recent business update, along with share price trend.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rail Vikas Nigam shares extend losing streak to fifth session, drop over 3.5% to 2-year low
articles.summary: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited shares fell 3% on June 2, extending a sell-off to five sessions, with a cumulative loss of 14.5%. The stock's year-to-date losses reached 33%, marking its steepest fall since 2019
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oracle layoffs: 30,000 jobs cuts to be completed by June 15 despite booming cloud, AI growth and strong earnings
articles.summary: Oracle layoffs: Executives have indicated that the layoffs are part of an effort to redirect resources toward high-growth AI businesses as demand for computing capacity surges, a report said.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Infosys share price jumps 5% in trade today; here's why
articles.summary: Infosys shares rose over 6% following the launch of Editorial Link Intelligence (ELI), an AI-driven tool designed for enhanced digital journalism in collaboration with Handelsblatt Media Group. ELI aims to improve editorial efficiency and reader engagement through content recommendations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Anthropic IPO: AI giant's $965 billion valuation faces market test; experts decode
articles.summary: AI company Anthropic has confidentially filed for an IPO in the US, marking a significant moment for the AI industry. The move tests if investor enthusiasm can sustain in public markets, potentially establishing valuation benchmarks amid a resurgence in IPO activity.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Banking sector Q4 results: NIMs likely bottomed out, business momentum improves; Axis Bank, DCB Bank among top picks
articles.summary: Equirus Securities expects repo hikes in the second half of FY27, which would be NIM supportive for private banks. While it sees reasonable upside across all large private banks, it prefers Axis Bank within that pack. Within mid-private banks, it likes Karur Vysya Bank and DCB Bank.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vadodara eyes India’s first public blue municipal bond issue, plans ₹200 crore fundraising
articles.summary: Gujarat’s Vadodara Municipal Corporation aims to raise up to ₹200 crore through a public blue bond issue for water-management projects, potentially becoming the first issuer to bring the sustainable-finance instrument to the Indian market.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Patanjali Foods: Street’s optimism in assigning high FMCG valuation multiples was misplaced
articles.summary: Patanjali targets FY27 volume growth of 3-5% in edible oils, 8-10% in foods, and 15% in home care. However, ICICI Securities cut its EPS estimate by 14%, forecasting ongoing earnings pressure due to rising input costs and stagnant profitability in the edible oils segment.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO: 10 key risks investors should know before subscribing; check latest GMP
articles.summary: Investors in Hexagon Nutrition's IPO should weigh risks such as heavy reliance on premix formulations, exposure to currency fluctuations, and challenges from international operations, among others before subscribing to the issue.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: GMR Airports faces West Asia headwinds, but analysts see a growth runway
articles.summary: Analysts expect traffic recovery from the second half of FY27, commissioning of Bhogapuram airport in Q2FY27, and the addition of Nagpur airport could support medium-term growth, analyst said
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vedanta shares crash over 4% from day's high after ED raids premises; details here
articles.summary: Vedanta Limited shares surged 71% in six months and 112% in a year, despite a turbulent market. However, shares fell over 4% today following raids by the Enforcement Directorate.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Zydus Lifesciences share buyback to open on 4 June: Should you participate or not?
articles.summary: Ahead of the buyback opening, Zydus Life stock was trading 2.5% lower at ₹1,063 apiece on Tuesday, 2 June. Here are the buyback details, along with experts' opinions on whether retail investors should participate.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: NSE SME stock close to 52-week high despite muted trends in Indian stock market
articles.summary: Shreeji Global FMCG share price remains close to a 52-week high, buoyed by a record profit of ₹19.92 crore for FY26, a 62.83% increase. The company also reported an 18.01% revenue rise, expanding its product lineup and sourcing network, despite broader market challenges.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: NHPC share price tanks around 6% after fundraise move through the OFS; check floor price and other details
articles.summary: NHPC shares fell nearly 6% following the Indian government's announcement to sell a 6% stake through an offer for sale. The move is part of a disinvestment strategy, with shares priced at ₹71 each, allowing retail and non-retail investors to participate.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Small-cap stocks to buy: Chalet Hotels, Minda Corp, Navin Fluorine among Axis Securities’ top stock picks for June
articles.summary: Small-cap stocks to buy: The smallcap stock picks for June are Ujjivan Small Finance Bank, Chalet Hotels, Minda Corporation, Navin Fluorine International and Kalpataru Projects International shares.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: IPO GMPs: CMR Green Technologies IPO to Hexagon Nutrition IPO; here's what GMP hints ahead of opening
articles.summary: IPO GMPs: The IPO market in India has cooled down, impacted by geopolitical and economic factors. CMR Green Technologies and Hexagon Nutrition are set to launch their IPOs, with CMR's grey market premium showing promise, while Hexagon's remains flat.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50 has ended June higher in 6 of the last 10 years. Will history repeat itself?
articles.summary: Crude oil trajectory holds the key for dictating stock market trend in June. As we enter this month, markets around the world are celebrating a 19% monthly decline in crude prices.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: ED searches against Vedanta Group in FEMA case
articles.summary: It is understood that the raids were launched against the business house, promoted by billionaire Anil Agarwal, on Monday.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Small-cap stock under ₹50 jumps despite weak trends on Dalal Street
articles.summary: Small-cap stock Blue Cloud Softech opened at ₹18.40 apiece today, as compared to previous close of ₹18 on Monday. Here's what's behind the stock rally, along with the share price trend.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Coca-Cola mulls IPO of its India bottling unit in 2027: Report
articles.summary: Coca-Cola counts India as a major growth market. It reported sales of 50 billion rupees ($526.37 million) in 2024-25, its highest since at least 2021.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50 Scorecard: The winners and losers of Q4 results season
articles.summary: Nifty EPS for FY26 ended at ₹1,065, up 5% YoY, a second consecutive year of single-digit growth. Five Nifty companies – Bharti Airtel, JSW Steel, HDFC Bank, Infosys, and Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) – contributed 75% of the incremental YoY accretion in earnings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Asian markets today: Air China, China Southern Airlines to Cathay Pacific Airways — why are aviation stocks falling?
articles.summary: Asian aviation stocks fell after Russia announced a temporary ban on aviation fuel exports, raising concerns about fuel supply stability and airline operating costs. Major airlines in China and Hong Kong experienced significant declines.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Lacklustre debut! Yaashvi Jewellers shares list at IPO price of ₹83 on BSE SME
articles.summary: Yaashvi Jewellers stock opened at ₹83 on BSE SME on Tuesday. This means that the IPO allottees made neither gain or loss over the listing of Yaashvi Jewellers IPO.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: KRN Heat Exchanger share price recovers 8% from day's low after firm raises ₹350 crore through QIP
articles.summary: KRN Heat Exchanger and Refrigeration recovered 6% after announcing a QIP that raised nearly ₹350 crore. The stock fell to ₹1,012.50 before rising to ₹1,092. Despite a 17% drop in a month, it gained 42% over the past year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: ACME Solar share price jumps 3% as firm launches QIP at ₹294.13 floor price
articles.summary: ACME Solar Holdings share price increased over 3% on June 2 after announcing a Qualified Institutional Placement (QIP) with a floor price of ₹294.13. The company aims to raise capital from institutional investors as it expands its renewable energy portfolio.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy: Sagar Doshi suggests Cyient, Timken India, JBM Auto shares to buy
articles.summary: Indian equity markets opened lower on June 2, with the Nifty 50 down 0.66% and the Sensex falling 0.43%. Concerns over the Middle East conflict and foreign investor selling contributed to the decline, with significant losses in major sectoral indices and the broader market.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Indian stock market slips to 7th spot in global market-cap rankings as South Korea overtakes on AI-led rally
articles.summary: According to Bloomberg data, the total market cap of Korea-listed companies has jumped to $5 trillion, while India’s has declined to $4.8 trillion. The sustained AI trade has powered the Korean stock market's 85% valuation surge this year.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Pimco Says Treasury Yields Driven by Fed Bets, Not AI, for Now
articles.summary: A boom in artificial intelligence-related borrowing may become a bigger influence on bond markets over time, but the idea that it’s behind a recent rise in long-dated Treasury yields appears overstated, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Aviation stocks under pressure: IndiGo and SpiceJet decline up to 2.5% as Russia bans jet fuel exports
articles.summary: Shares of InterGlobe Aviation and SpiceJet fell on June 2 due to Russia's ban on aviation fuel exports until November 2026, affecting investor sentiment. SpiceJet dropped 2.5%, while InterGlobe fell over 1%.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Ola Electric share price falls 2% as QIP issue priced at a discount. Do you own?
articles.summary: Electric two-wheeler manufacturer Ola Electric opened at ₹38.40 apiece today, compared with the previous close of ₹39.53 on BSE. Here are the QIP details, along with the share price trend.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices steady after 5% surge amid ongoing US-Iran talks; Brent near $95/bbl. What's the outlook?
articles.summary: US-Iran war: Crude oil prices on Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) witnessed a downward movement. MCX crude oil prices fell as much as 0.16% to ₹8,725 per barrel.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold rate today: MCX gold price rises above ₹1.59 lakh per 10 grams, silver flat amid uncertainty over US-Iran talks
articles.summary: Gold rate today: Gold prices in the international market were steady as investors awaited further details on the status of the US-Iran talks amid conflicting news.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: US Spot Petrochemical Prices Ease as Export Demand Starts to Lag
articles.summary: US spot petrochemical prices softened across the board last week as export demand for the plastics building blocks appears to weaken for the month of June.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Silver rate today: Silver prices remain muted as markets assess US-Iran news and Fed outlook - Key levels to watch
articles.summary: MCX silver price was muted, down 0.17% at ₹2,65,685 per kg, while MCX gold price also flat.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Rupee opens 7 paise lower at 95.06 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened at 95.06 against the US dollar, weakened by crude oil price volatility and foreign investor selling. Despite nearly $25 billion in outflows, the rupee has remained stable due to central bank interventions. Market focus now shifts to RBI policy and US-Iran negotiations.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Centre’s domestic financing nearly doubles to ₹3.6 trillion in April, easing capital spending pressure
articles.summary: Strong small savings inflows and residual domestic financing provide a cushion for the government's FY27 spending, reducing its reliance on market borrowings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India's fertility problem may be worse than what headline figures suggest
articles.summary: Driven by changing societal aspirations, India's fertility rate has plunged below replacement levels, particularly in southern states. The desire to have more children is even less.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India GDP growth likely slowed to 7.3% in Q4 FY26: Mint poll
articles.summary: Domestic demand and government spending likely kept growth resilient in the January-March quarter despite headwinds from the West Asia war.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt trims fertilizer demand estimate after IMD downgrades monsoon forecast
articles.summary: Stocks currently stand at 19.98 million tonnes, more than 52% of the revised seasonal requirement, significantly ahead of the typical 33% benchmark at this time in the season.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Industrial output grows 4.9% in April as India shifts to new IIP series with 2022-23 base
articles.summary: The update marks the first official reading under a revised base year, which adds high-tech products and energy-related services to better reflect the modern economy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s manufacturing PMI climbs to 55.0 in May as output hits three-month high
articles.summary: Driven by domestic demand resilience, Indian factory activity outperformed estimates in May as new orders and production grew at their fastest pace since February despite persistent cost pressures.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: GST collections rise 3.2% to ₹1.94 trillion in May
articles.summary: Excluding the impact of a one-time ₹10,000-crore GST payment made by a telecom operator for spectrum allocation in May 2025, gross collections recorded an adjusted growth of 9% during May
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Powell says Fed will lose credibility if president removes officials over policy differences
articles.summary: The remarks were his first since stepping down as Federal Reserve chair.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt to help 150,000 farmers access machinery, curb stubble burning
articles.summary: The government aims to support 150,000 small farmers with ₹8,550 crore for farm equipment access, promoting mechanisation and reducing stubble burning through the establishment of custom hiring centres, enhancing productivity and resource efficiency in agriculture.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rain, thunderstorm seen in many parts, conditions favour monsoon progress: IMD
articles.summary: The IMD has forecast heavy rain in parts of northeast and south India, along with severe thunderstorms, hailstorms and winds up to 90 kmph across several regions. As heatwave conditions ease, southwest monsoon is expected to advance further over Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Lakshadweep and adjoining seas.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is 2026 heading for its driest monsoon since 2015?
articles.summary: IMD has lowered its monsoon outlook to 90% of normal, raising concerns over heatwaves, farm output, and food prices as El Niño risks loom large.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: How the stress is building in real estate globally, explained in five charts
articles.summary: After soaring in the wake of the covid pandemic, real estate prices globally have been cooling down. The trickle-down effects of the US-Iran war could prolong that.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt warns of fresh inflation risks: Fuel prices, monsoon to further squeeze your household budget
articles.summary: The finance ministry's report comes just days ahead of RBI’s Monetary Policy decision scheduled to be announced next week.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India eases fuel export levies, lowers duties on petrol, diesel and ATF starting June 1
articles.summary: The duty on exports of petrol has been set at ₹1.5 ($0.0158) per litre while that on diesel has been set at ₹13.5 per litre. Export duties on ATF have been set at ₹9.5 per litre.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will AI put economists out of business? Fed’s John Williams says 'I’m not a believer that...'
articles.summary: The implications of artificial intelligence gripped global central bankers in Iceland this week, with one Federal Reserve official joking that it won’t put economists out of business.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Households’ health bills, emerging markets tracker, childcare gap
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India opens CSR funding to non-profits via social stock exchange in landmark policy shift
articles.summary: India government has allowed companies to invest up to 10 per cent of their CSR funds in zero coupon zero principal instruments issued by not for profit organisations through a social stock exchange
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI balance sheet jumps 20.6% on gold rally, rupee depreciation
articles.summary: Liquidity infusion through RBI’s open market purchases of government securities was also a factor for the expansion in the balance sheet
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI says West Asia war clouds growth outlook, but India remains relatively resilient
articles.summary: RBI cautioned that a prolonged escalation of the war could hurt growth momentum and rekindle inflationary pressures through surging crude oil prices, volatile commodity markets and disruptions in shipping routes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India to get 'plastic notes'? RBI considers decade-old plan. What are polymer banknotes and are they better than paper?
articles.summary: Reserve Bank of India is reportedly reviving a decade-old plan to introduce polymer banknotes, with a pilot project expected soon. Here is what plastic currency is, why the RBI wants it and which countries already use it.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India is delaying its key Q4 GDP data release by a week
articles.summary: The statistics ministry has shifted the release of Q4 GDP data from 31 May to 7 June each year. This year, the data will be released on 5 June, since 7 June is a Sunday.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: World’s rich pocket $1 billion with big sales of surging stocks
articles.summary: The transactions offer a snapshot into how wealthy private investors are locking in returns in larger positions as global stock indexes hit record highs despite geopolitical turmoil, fueled by enthusiasm for AI and rising corporate earnings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India sees rising evening power shortages as solar supply fades
articles.summary: India's power grid faces challenges as evening demand surges after sunset, leading to widespread outages. A shortage of thermal power and reliance on spot markets contribute to the crisis, particularly in northern and western regions, with demand predicted to rise further due to agricultural needs.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Indonesia’s nickel woes due to West Asia war to hit India’s EV battery and stainless-steel sectors
articles.summary: India's electric vehicle and stainless-steel sectors face rising nickel prices and supply uncertainties due to the West Asia war. The country's heavy reliance on nickel imports, particularly from Indonesia, poses risks of increased costs and planning uncertainties for manufacturers and consumers.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Why India wants to go back to standard edible oil pack sizes
articles.summary: The government plans to bring back standard pack sizes for edible oils on complaints that similar-looking pouches with different quantities and prices were making value-for-money comparisons difficult for buyers. The move aims to improve transparency and ease shopping choices.
articles.label: NEUTRAL
